methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $23M total raised proxy.

private.name: Cover
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Cuby Technologies is a Series B-stage frontier-tech company in Housing & Construction founded by Aleks Gampel and Aleh Kandrashou. The company has raised $23.4M to date. The comparable universe is sparse: one private peer (Cover) and no public comparables in the same sector, which constrains triangulation depth.

Given the limited comp set, valuation relies primarily on stage-progression methodology. Using total capital raised ($23.4M) as a baseline proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $44M. This reflects empirical step-up patterns observed in frontier-tech funding rounds at equivalent maturity stages.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $38M to $51M, with a median point estimate of $44M. The 35% band width reflects reasonable upside/downside around stage-typical progression but should be interpreted cautiously: the single-method framework and minimal peer reference set create material uncertainty. Key variables influencing true value include unit economics, revenue traction, market size defensibility, and capital efficiency relative to the Cover comparable—none of which are quantified here. Additional private comps or revenue multiples would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
